commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an internal command metadata callback, shares Gateway `sessions.changed` emission with `chat.send`, wires `/goal` and command session-store writes to notify subscribers, and updates docs plus targeted tests.

PR surface: Source +183, Tests +175, Docs +2. Total +360 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main `/goal` writes session goal metadata without any Gateway subscriber callback, while `sessions.changed` emission is scoped to session RPC/lifecycle paths. I did not run a live browser repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof, which automation cannot provide for their setup.

target.title: Emit sessions.changed for in-chat command metadata
target.body:
Fixes #88598

## User-facing bug

Control UI clients subscribed to session updates could miss metadata changes made by in-chat commands. A command-only turn such as `/goal complete`, `/goal pause`, or `/goal clear` can update the session store without starting a model run, but the Gateway did not emit a `sessions.changed` event for that mutation. The visible session row could keep stale goal/status metadata until a manual reload or another unrelated session event.

## Exact repro

Source-level reproduction:

1. Connect a Control UI/WebChat client that subscribes to `sessions.changed`.
2. Send an in-chat `/goal` command that mutates the persisted session goal, for example `/goal complete shipped`.
3. The command handler writes the updated goal to the session store and returns command output.
4. Before this patch, no command-metadata `sessions.changed` event was broadcast for the store write, so subscribed clients did not get a scoped refresh for that session.

## Fix summary

- Add a typed `onSessionMetadataChanged` reply option for command handlers.
- Notify after successful command-session store writes through the shared `persistSessionEntry` helper.
- Notify `/goal` start, pause, resume, complete, blocked, clear, and status paths when they actually change the persisted goal metadata.
- Share the Gateway `sessions.changed` emitter between session RPC handlers and `chat.send`, so command-only chat turns can broadcast a scoped session row update.
- Delay command-metadata broadcasts until after the current command turn leaves the active-run map, while still preserving `hasActiveRun: true` if another run for the same session is active.
- Document that command-only session metadata changes refresh subscribed clients.

## Targeted tests

- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for metadata-change notifications on mutating goal actions and no-op clear paths.
- Added Gateway sessions coverage for command-metadata `sessions.changed` payloads and active-run exclusion.
- Added Gateway chat coverage proving command-only `/goal` turns broadcast scoped session updates and do not report their own command turn as active.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: In-chat command metadata writes now emit scoped `sessions.changed` events, so subscribed Control UI/WebChat clients can refresh visible session metadata after command-only turns.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/sessions-changed-in-chat-commands`, commit `3623771e`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 docs/tools/slash-commands.md src/auto-reply/get-reply-options.types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-goal.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-goal.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-store.ts src/auto-reply/types.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server-methods/session-change-events.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts`
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 --reporter verbose src/auto-reply/reply/commands-goal.test.ts`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 --reporter verbose src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 --reporter verbose src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- `commands-goal.test.ts` passed: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 10 passed (10)`.
- `server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts` passed: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 14 passed (14)`.
- `server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` passed: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 30 passed (30)`.
- `node scripts/docs-list.js` and `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` found no discrete regression that would block the patch.

Observed result after fix: Command-only `/goal` metadata mutations produce scoped `sessions.changed` broadcasts with fresh session row metadata, while the command's own `chat.send` abort controller is not reported as an active model run.

What was not tested: Full core typecheck is still blocked by pre-existing `ui/src/ui/views/workboard-3d-game.ts` errors for missing `three` type resolution and an implicit `any`. I did not run a live browser Control UI session against a real long-running `/goal` command. I did not implement or test `/name` because this checkout does not contain a `/name` handler.

## Not tested / known gaps

- This patch focuses on command metadata changes that happen through `chat.send` reply options and the shared command-session store helper.
- Existing channel-only command flows that do not pass a Gateway session subscriber callback are unchanged.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level reproduction, targeted regression tests, and `codex review --base origin/main` to validate the behavior above.
